event: admitted to the Department of Urology
minimal blood urea nitrogen (BUN) increase (63 mg/dl)
urine culture infection from Candida albicans (5 × 105CFU/ml)
antimycotic treatment with fluconazole (200 mg twice a day)
wide spectrum antibiotic coverage with meropenem (1 g three times a day)
abdominal ultrasound showing multiple bilateral kidney stones
weakness in right arm and leg
admission CT scan showing left parietal epidural mass
left lateral ventricle displacement (midline shift: 7 mm)
MRI showing ipsilateral hemispheric leptomeningeal enhancement
large discoid mass involving dura mater
durotomy started 2 cm beyond lesion border
CT and MRI confirming full lesion removal
antimycotic treatment with amphotericin B (3 mg/kg a day)
enhanced CT showing subcutaneous and epidural empyema
no complications at 6-12-24 months’ neuroradiological investigations
